What   wi l l   we  cover   t oday?


Explore ‘Information Systems Management’ in terms of necessity, key concepts,
and contemporary trends


Focus specifically on the nature, use, benefits and drawbacks of ‘Outsourcing’


Look into the ‘Green Technology’ concept and how it links to the SDGs
(Sustainable Development Goals)

ISM
Information Systems Management

Why  st udy  i nfor mat i on  syst ems  management ?


Until now, we’ve looked mainly at the key components and operating environments of
information systems


The information systems field also concentrates on the management            of the systems


This is because these critical and costly systems do not manage themselves: technology changes
+ contextual environments change













                                                                                                                             5

What   does  I SM  I nvol ve?


Managing IS to innovate     their operations    and achieve competitive advantage


Managing IS to support     business processes     and decision-making


Managing IS to enable     digital busines models and social engagement


Managing IS development methods          such SDLC    to deploy technology solutions


Managing the Human Resource (HR) requirements of there IS/IT portfolio


Managing IS to operationalize, coordinate and monitor their supply chains


Managing IS infrastructure     to enable business applications and processes


Managing IS to effectively manage customer relationships                                                                            6

7
     What   about   t he  ext er nal   envi r onment ?







                                                                                               Can we manage it?


                                                                                                 Does it manage
                                                                                                        us?

                                                                                                   compare to
                                                                                                    Porter ’s 5?

Major  responsi bi l i ti es  of  the  IT  functi on?


Keep the lights on


IS/ IT planning to accomplish organizational goals and strategies


Protect  technology and data assets


Develop, operate, maintain and re-fresh the organisation’s              technology infrastructure


Develop, operate, maintain, re-fresh the organisation’s            applications


Management of outsourcing          re l at i ons hi p s


Management of internal stakeholder re q ui re me nt s   and   re l at i ons hi p s


….be on top of emerging issues                                                                                                             8

Outsourcing and
Offshoring

What   i s  out sour ci ng?


’A    l    l    o    c    a    t    i    o    n        o    f     specific business processes to a specialist ex t e r nal   se r v i ce   p rov i d e r’


Almost every organization outsources in some way


Ty p i c a l l y,   t h e   f u n c t i o n   b e i n g   o u t s o u rc e d   i s  considered non-core to the business


Full or near complete outsourcing = approximately 80% or more of a business’s requirements are
purposely supported by an external entity.











                                                                                                                                                                               10

What   ar eas  ar e  most   commonl y  out sour ced?


IT outsourcing
Legal outsourcing
Content Development
We b   D e s i g n   a n d   M a i n t e n a n c e
Recruitment
Logistics
Manufacturing
Te     c     h    n    i     c     a     l     /     C     u    s     t     o     m    e     r           S    u    p     p     o     r     t










                                                                                                                                                                                                        11

How di d the cost i mperati ve dri ve outsourci ng?


A key management goal from the 1990s onwards has been ‘costs–out ’


Example: Australian Banks spend over a AUD$ 1-          2 Billion on IT annually. If you can take 5% off
that – that ’s about $AUD 100 Mill


Organisations looked to commoditise        services (e.g. data storage) to reduce costs


Gave rise to outsourcing providers and offshoring centres (in countries with inexpensive labour
costs)










                                                                                                                                  12

What   el se  dr ove  out sour ci ng?


Lack of the necessary IT/network skills to complete or support projects


Lack of the necessary design skills


Infrequent requirements: i.e. not worth training existing staff


Proprietary equipment or software: Hardware/software contractual obligations to only use
specifically certified personnel from a designated supplier


Remain competitive through the ability to access the required resources, competencies and
capabilities, to deliver value back to customers (clients)

What   ar e  t he  cost   advant ages?


Focus on core areas


Save on infrastructure and technology


Access to skilled resources


Time zone advantage


Faster and better services









                                                                                                                             14

15
      Can we outsource too much?


      Survey of nearly 1,000 small businesses (US based) and found that technology plays a major role
      in their growth


      70% of respondents said that technology is key to their success


      41% said technology needs are becoming increasingly complex


      When IT/IS becomes core business or core to competitive advantage, might need to rethink
      outsourcing arrangements (e.g. think of move to online shopping)






















      Ref: Vanderveldt, I 2013, Understanding the State of Local Startupsand Small Businesses, Dell Inc.

What   ar e  ot her   r i sks  wi t h  out sour ci ng?


Risk of exposing confidential data and IP: Outsourcing HR, Payroll and Recruitment services,
ex p ose s  confi d e nt i al   comp any   i nfor mat i on  t o  a  t hi rd-party.


Synchronizing the deliverables: the wrong partner for outsourcing can result in stretched
delivery time frames, sub-standard quality output and inappropriate categorization of
re s p ons i b i l i t i e s


Hidden costs: Signing international contracts can result in unexpected outcomes.


Lack of customer focus: vendor may be catering to the expertise-needs of multiple organizations
at a time








                                                                                                                                                 16

What   i s  t he  ‘aaS’ model?                                                                                                 Also see
                                                                                                                              Week 6


Subscription business models are based on the idea of requiring a product or service (or both) by
paying a monthly or yearly recurring subscription revenue.


With the rise of technology and software as a service (SaaS) products, many companies are
moving from a business revenue model where revenue is made from a customer  's one-time
purchase to a subscription model where revenue is made on a re cur r i ng   b as i s















                                                                                                                                         17

How do we ensure qual i ty wi th outsourci ng?


A service level agreement (SLA)          is a contract between a service provider (either internal or
ex t e r nal )   and   t he   e nd   use r   t hat   d e fi ne s  t he   l ev e l   of  se r v i ce   ex p e ct e d   from  t he   se r v i ce   p rov i d e r .


SLA Metrics include
A description of the service being provided
Reliability
Responsiveness
Procedure for reporting problems
Monitoring and reporting service level
Consequences for not meeting service obligations
Escape clauses or constraints
…




                                                                                                                                                     18

What   i s  offshor i ng?


Ta      k    e      s             a      d      v      a      n      t      a      g      e             o      f       the cost differentials by relocating factories and services from costly
countries to the cheaper economies in order to sell at a greater profit.




















                                                                                                                                                                                                                 19

When  offshor i ng,   what   must   we  be  awar e  of?


Local market awareness –         when dealing with clients that may have specific needs and special
re q ui re me nt s   out s i d e   of   t he   nor mal .   A n  ex t e r nal   cons ul t ant   may   b e   ne e d e d   t o  b r i d g e   t he   g ap   of
awareness between the client and the network developers.


Local conditions awareness –          When dealing with unusually physical environments and
constraints a consultant may be required to clarify how the physical conditions may impact on
the performance and reliability of the network.


Information integrity –      when dealing with the transmission of confidential or information that
could be of a very sensitive nature, the need for a network security specialist with a high level of
security access maybe a mandatory requirement for such projects.









                                                                                                                                                 20

Green IT
The SDGs and the Circular Economy

What   i s  Gr een  Technol ogy?


The use of technology to make products and
processes more environmentally friendly


This means looking at the individual components of
information systems and seeking ways to re d uce
environmental footprints


It also involves using information systems to monitor
and/or re-design other business systems to make
them more sustainable


Examples include re d uci ng   CO 2   e mi s s i ons  or by
making products more biodegradable












https://link.springer.com/chapter/10.1007/978-3-030-48141-4_9
                                                                                                                                                22

Examples: https://www.greenit.net/services.html

What   i s  sust ai nabi l i t y?  What   ar e  t he  SDGs?


Environmental sustainability = the heart
of green IT is the concept


Sustainability = "development that meets
the needs of the present without
compromising the ability of future
generations to meet their own needs."


SDGs  = Sustainable Development Goals,
part of the global agenda to achieve
sustainability outcomes by 2030, through
the achievement of 17 goals


Achievement is heavily reliant on smart
technology application







                                                                                                                24                    24

How does IS support carbon and energy tracki ng?


Energy management is the creation of technology to capture data about a company ’s carbon
emissions


Majority of these tools focus on the actual use of energy by the company

















                                                                                                                     25

26
      What   ar e  i nt el l i gent   suppl y  chai ns?


      IT plays a key role: carbon accounting down the supply chain


      Collier (2010) states that “according to industry re p or t s   7 5 %  of   a
      company  's carbon footprint derives from transportation and
      logistics.


      Ethical implications for retailers and e-commerce businesses,
      down the chain –       and Corporate Social Responsibility


      Optimize operations through analyzing              and optimizing the supply
      chain through an environmental lens

















      https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=UtBa9yVZBJM

27
      Does ‘ li fe cycle’  thi nki ng help?


      IT domain now talks in terms of systems life cycles  and
      information ecologies


      These terms are taken from the ecological and
      biological sciences


      The idea is to analogise IT with living systems with the
      aim that we start managing them in a similar way


      Particularly the ‘end of life’ phase i.e. factor that into
      design and renewal. Don’t just chuck


      This approach demands that approaches to accounting,
      financing, and costing     are modified
